name: Discord
description: A real-time chat platform for communities and developers offering text/voice/video, servers (guilds), channels, and a programmable public API for bots and integrations.

auth_methods.note: Discord supports OAuth2 flows (authorization code, PKCE, client credentials, etc.). OAuth token exchanges use HTTP Basic auth (client_id:client_secret base64) for some flows, and bots authenticate to the REST API using a Bot token sent in the Authorization header (Authorization: Bot <token>). Evidence: OAuth2 docs and Applications dashboard docs.

credential_access.note: Developers can create applications and add bot users in the Developer Portal to obtain Client ID, Client Secret, and Bot Token without human approval or paid plans.

api_surface.note: Discord provides a broad REST API covering channels, guilds, users, messages, etc., plus a real-time Gateway (WebSocket) for events (both documented in the Developer Docs).

buildability.note: Public docs and self-serve credential creation exist, allowing developers to build today. Operational considerations: bots above certain scale or accessing privileged data (e.g., GUILD_MEMBERS, PRESENCE) require intent enablement and verification (human review) as described in the verification and privileged intents docs, but these do not prevent initial development.

queries: Discord developer applications create app bot token client secret
